content.claim: Between-school socio-economic variance in Hungary is among the highest in the OECD (23.1% vs. OECD average 14.8%), and between-school variance in Hungary ranks third-highest overall while within-school variance remains below OECD average, indicating that tracking concentrates socio-economic disparity at school level.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: OECD data does not isolate causal contribution of age-10/12 tracking versus other system features
Cross-sectional data cannot establish counterfactual outcomes under alternative tracking ages
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
